backlog-claim.note: openâ†’active via the real applyTransition(before, "claim", opts); kept only if replay reproduces the historical after byte-for-byte.

backlog-resolve.note: active|openâ†’resolved via applyTransition(before, "resolve", opts), including graduatedTo/codifiedIn where the historical commit set them.

backlog-settle.note: born-active scaffoldâ†’open; replays the inline transform scripts/backlog.mjs#settle uses (not exported there as a pure function).

backlog-created.note: a backlog item's first-appearance revision (before === null) â€” structural fixtures for scaffold-shape regressions.

memory.note: MEMORY.md index + agent-memory-src/*.md per-entry rule adds/changes (current and pre-#2266 legacy .claude/agent-memory/ homes aliased together).

hook-locus-prefix.note: a backlog/reports edit that FIXES a bare code-path ref; scanRepoLocusPrefixes flags `before` and is clean on `after` for the same file.

hook-guard-bash.note: guard-bash denies BEFORE a commit exists, so no historical "denied" commit is mineable; each case is grounded in an incident documented in guard-bash.mjs's own header comment, run through the real exported decide() at mine time.

hook-guard-lane.note: same "fires pre-commit" gap as guard-bash, PLUS its input is a filesystem path classified against the real ~/workspace layout â€” not portable as a literal path. Fixtures carry {{PRIMARY_ROOT}}/{{LANE_ROOT}}/{{SCRATCHPAD_ROOT}} placeholders for the replaying harness to substitute.
